text.title: Sprint Capacity From Complexity Tags Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the sprint-capacity-from-complexity-tags methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale. The terminal `skip-this-methodology` rule is the safety valve invoked by the decision tree when preconditions fail.

rule.statement: Allowed tags MUST be exactly XS, S, M, L, XL — no custom letters.
rule.rationale: Custom tags fragment calibration and prevent cross-team comparisons.

rule.statement: Capacity model MUST express output in slots, not hours / days.
rule.rationale: Time units re-introduce the failure mode this methodology removes.

rule.statement: Calibration MUST use trailing 3 sprints; older data ignored.
rule.rationale: Older data biases weights toward outdated practices.

rule.statement: When a sprint's planned slot sum > capacity, planner MUST refuse the sprint.
After each sprint, weights MUST be recalibrated and committed.
rule.rationale: Stale weights mislead planning within 1 quarter.

rule.statement: Author MUST stop and route elsewhere when the decision-tree resolves to a skip leaf; partial application of this methodology against an unfit input is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Forcing the methodology onto a mismatched input produces low-quality artefacts and erodes trust in the corpus.
